title: Jutka
content:
{{Top|ffa|leg}} {{Character |type= |image= |name=Jutka |homeworld=[[Dayark]]{{CSWECite|II|184}} |birth= |death= |species=[[Tuhgri]] |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Male]]{{DarkStryder|adventure=Galaxy's Edge|book=The Kathol Outback}} |pronouns=new |height= |mass= |hair= |feathers= |eyes= |skin= |cyber= |families=new |parents=new |partners=new |siblings=new |children=new |affiliation=[[Kathol Republic]]''[[Vision of the Future]]'' |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|My job is to protect the Kathol Republic. I have no such responsibility toward outsiders who come in unasked and meddle with matters that are none of their business.|Jutka to Talon Karrde and Shada D'ukal|}} '''Jutka''' was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Tuhgri]] from the [[Moon/Legends|moon]] [[Dayark]] in the [[Kathol Outback/Legends|Kathol Outback]]. In [[8 ABY/Legends|8 ABY]], he was [[Commander/Legends|commander]] of a small [[Starship/Legends|starship]]. On route to the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Danoor]] in the [[Nah'Malis system]], Jutka's ship was forced to make an emergency maneuver when an [[Aing-Tii/Legends|Aing-Tii]] [[Sanhedrim ship]] dropped out of [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] in front of them. Skimming through an energy tendril from the nearby [[Kathol Rift/Legends|Kathol Rift]]—a dense gas cloud fraught with [[lightstorm]]s—the ship's controls were completely ionized, forcing the eight-being crew to abandon ship in a single [[Escape pod/Legends|escape pod]]. Drifting in an escape pod, Jutka detected a faint energy reading in the [[Duhrib Belt]], an [[Asteroid field/Legends|asteroid field]] orbiting between the [[Star system/Legends|system's]] fourth and fifth planets. Tracking the signal, the Tuhgri discovered a damaged [[Mining drone/Legends|mining drone]] that Jutka hoped could be used to signal their [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]], or attract rescuers to their plight. During their repair operations, the Tuhgri met representatives from the crew of the ''[[FarStar]]'', a [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] vessel, who were engaged in recovering the drone for the government of Danoor. Working together, they salvaged the drone, and Jutka and his crew escaped the [[Asteroid/Legends|asteroid]]. Eleven years later, in [[19 ABY/Legends|19 ABY]], Jutka served the [[Kathol Republic]] as a [[General/Legends|general]] and commander of its [[Kathol Republic Navy|armed forces]]. Following a skirmish between [[Talon Karrde]]'s ''[[Wild Karrde]]'' and [[Corsair-class cruiser|''Corsair''-class assault starfighters]] belonging to the [[Rodian/Legends|Rodian]] [[Pirate/Legends|pirate]] [[Rei'Kas]] in the [[Episol system]], Jutka met with Karrde. He warned the [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggler]] that the Kathol Republic wanted no part in the war between Rei'Kas and [[Crev Bombaasa/Legends|Crev Bombaasa]]—under whose protection Karrde was flying—and that the Rodian would not stop hunting Karrde while he was in his territory. ==Biography== ===Incident at Danoor=== A native of [[Dayark]] within the region of [[Realspace/Legends|space]] known as the [[Kathol Outback/Legends|Kathol Outback]], the [[Tuhgri]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] Jutka [[Commander/Legends|commanded]] a [[Starship/Legends|starship]] crewed by seven of his fellow Tuhgri. In [[8 ABY/Legends|8 ABY]], the ship was heading for the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Danoor]] in the [[Nah'Malis system]] on the edge of the [[Kathol Rift/Legends|Kathol Rift]], a violent and turbulent area consisting of dense gas clouds, energy streamers and [[lightstorm]]s. As the vessel skirted the edge of the Rift, an [[Aing-Tii/Legends|Aing-Tii]] [[Sanhedrim ship]] emerged from [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] directly in their path. Forced to make an evasive maneuver, the vessel came in contact with one of the Rift's tendrils. The energy discharge ionized the ship's primary and secondary systems, rendering the vessel inoperable. As the ship drifted closer to the Rift, Jutka gave the order to abandon ship, and all eight crew members used the vessel's [[Escape pod/Legends|escape pod]]. The escape pod made it as far as the [[Duhrib Belt]], an [[Asteroid field/Legends|asteroid field]] orbiting between the [[Star system/Legends|system's]] fourth and fifth planets. Jutka detected an energy signature emanating from a [[Mining drone/Legends|mining drone]] used by the citizens of Danoor to mine the [[Asteroid/Legends|asteroids]] for raw materials and ores. Tracking the signal through the asteroids, the Tuhgri eventually lost the signal and had to scan for [[Durasteel/Legends|durasteel]] rather than energy readings. The readings led them to a massive asteroid at the heart of the belt, and they discovered that the drone was stuck in a crevasse. Descending into the crevasse, the Tuhgri found the drone, and Jutka hoped that they could use it to send a message back to their people, or—at the very least—attract the attention of rescuers who could get them to the nearest inhabited planet. However, the drone's [[repulsor power modulator]] was damaged, and they lacked the tools they needed to repair it, forcing them to cannibalize their escape pod to create makeshift tools. Donning [[Environment suit/Legends|vacuum suits]], Jutka and the Tuhgri began to repair the drone. ===Encounter with the ''FarStar''=== {{RPGseed}} [[File:Tughrianddrone.jpg|right|thumb|Jutka's Tuhgri work on the mining drone]] As the Tuhgri worked on the drone, Jutka became aware of tremors running through the asteroid, and theorized that they were caused by smaller asteroids impacting upon the one they were inside. During the repair process, Jutka and the Tuhgri left the mining drone to return to their escape pod. When they returned to the drone, they found a small group of strangers attempting to retrieve the drone. Initially, the Tuhgri charged at the group, brandishing their makeshift tools as weapons. However, when the new arrivals showed no intent of hostile action against them, Jutka ordered his crew to stand down and approached them. The new arrivals explained their presence—they were members of the crew of the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] [[CR90 corvette/Legends|CR90 corvette]] ''[[FarStar]]'', and were helping the government of Danoor in retrieving their drone in exchange for information on locating a navigator who could [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] their vessel through the Kathol Rift. In turn, Jutka explained the predicament that he and his crew found themselves in. Agreeing to work together, the Tuhgri and the ''FarStar'' party repaired the drone. As they finished the work, the asteroid tremors became more violent, beginning to crack the rock walls of the crevasse and sending debris raining down onto the two groups. The Tuhgri raced for the safety of the vessel that had ferried the ''FarStar''{{'s}} crew down into the crevasse—an [[Aegis-class Combat Shuttle|''Aegis''-class combat shuttle]]—along with their new allies. The shuttle lifted off, heading back to the surface of the asteroid to rendezvous with the [[Corvette/Legends|corvette]] ''FarStar''. On the way out, the shuttle pilot had to avoid falling slabs of rock, and use the [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]] cannons to clear debris from the crevasse's entrance. Jutka and his Tuhgri were then taken back to Danoor. {{Endgame}} ===Encounter with Talon Karrde=== [[File:KarrdeNJO.jpg|thumb|left|150px|Talon Karrde]] {{Quote|The fact is that you're a troublemaker. Troublemakers aren't welcome on my world.|Jutka to Talon Karrde|}} Eleven [[Standard year/Legends|years]] later, Jutka had joined the [[Kathol Republic]], holding the rank of [[General/Legends|general]] and serving as commander of its [[Kathol Republic Navy|armed forces]]. Based on Dayark in the [[Episol system]], Jutka kept a close eye on both [[Corellian/Legends|Corellian]] [[Crime lord/Legends|crime lord]] [[Crev Bombaasa/Legends|Crev Bombaasa's]] [[Bombaasa Cartel|cartel]], and the organization of the [[Rodian/Legends|Rodian]] [[Pirate/Legends|pirate]] [[Rei'Kas]]. He felt that both posed a threat to the stability of the Republic, as they were involved in a gang war. Following a skirmish involving the [[Action VI bulk transport/Legends|Action VI transport]] ''[[Wild Karrde]]'' and four of Rei'Kas's [[Corsair-class cruiser|''Corsair''-class assault starfighters]] in the system, Jutka requested to meet with the [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggler]] [[Talon Karrde]], owner of the transport. Jutka arranged to meet Karrde and his associate, the former [[Mistryl Shadow Guard]] [[Shada D'ukal]], in a [[Tapcafe/Legends|tapcafe]] near the [[Spaceport/Legends|spaceport]] in the capital city of [[Rytal Prime]]. For the meeting, Jutka dressed in civilian clothing, although he was not comfortable being out of uniform, and disliked the presence of the [[Protocol droid/Legends|protocol droid]] [[C-3PO/Legends|C-3PO]] at the meeting. He told the smuggler that the Kathol Republic wanted no part in the conflict that was occurring between Bombaasa's cartel and Rei'Kas's pirates. As Karrde's ship was equipped with a unique [[ID overlay]] that identified it as under Bombaasa's protection—Bombaasa had given it to him as payback for an incident several years earlier when two of his associates had saved Bombaasa's life—Rei'Kas had attacked the ship. Karrde was surprised to learn that Jutka knew the identity of Rei'Kas, as Bombaasa did not, despite the fact that it was his organization that was under attack. Jutka was worried that their conflict threatened to spill over into Kathol Republic territory, and warned Karrde that the Rodian would continue to pursue him all the time he was in Rei'Kas's territory. ==Personality and traits== [[File:Tuhgri.jpg|thumb|right|150px|A Tuhgri]] {{Quote|I thought I was here for a conversation, not a series of threats.''"
"''I apologize if you got that impression.|Talon Karrde and Jutka|}} Jutka was a capable commander, able to make snap decisions to save the lives of his crew and then devise a plan to have them rescued when they found themselves stranded in the Nah'Malis system. Although initially wary of the ''FarStar'' crew, Jutka was able to be diplomatic when he realized that they meant him and his crew no harm, and was able to convince the New Republic representatives to work with the Tuhgri towards a common goal. As a military officer, he was uncomfortable when out of uniform; on the occasion he met Talon Karrde in a tapcafe, he was wearing a [[Crosh-hide]] jacket that was the height of fashion on Dayark and showed visible signs of discomfort. An intimidating and menacing individual when the situation warranted it, Jutka was genuinely concerned for the safety of the Kathol Republic, and worked hard to ensure that they were not caught in the gang war between Bombaasa's cartel and Rei'Kas's pirates. He also harbored a dislike for [[Droid/Legends|droids]], ordering C-3PO—who was accompanying Karrde—to shut up or be forcibly shut down. ==Behind the scenes== Jutka was introduced in the [[Roleplaying game|RPG]] adventure ''[[Galaxy's Edge]]'', written by [[George R. Strayton]], which appeared in ''[[The Kathol Outback]]'', the second supplement for [[The DarkStryder Campaign (series)|''The DarkStryder Campaign'']]. A character named Jutka later appeared in ''[[Vision of the Future]]'' by [[Timothy Zahn]], although this Jutka was presented as a heavyset Human, rather than a Tuhgri. Both characters were, however, present in stories set in the Kathol Outback. In [[2008]], ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' finally established that both characters were actually the same individual, and that he was a Tuhgri despite his description as a Human in ''Vision of the Future''. In the adventure ''Galaxy's Edge'', it is possible for the crew of the ''FarStar'' to engage the Tuhgri in battle upon their first meeting. As the Tuhgri charge the crew with makeshift tools upon seeing them, the players have the option of taking aggressive action in response. If this option is taken, a fight immediately occurs. However, the adventure continues with the assumption that the ''FarStar'' crew and the Tuhgri are working together to salvage and repair the drone. ==Appearances== *{{DarkStryder|adventure=Galaxy's Edge|book=The Kathol Outback|reprint=yes}} {{1st}} *''[[Vision of the Future]]'' {{Ab|Vision of the Future (unabridged audiobook)}} ==Sources== *''[[The Kathol Outback]]'' *''[[The DarkStryder Campaign, Deluxe]]'' *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} [[Category:Admirals and generals]] [[Category:Males]] [[Category:Tuhgri]]
